adds.rationale: Rocket Lab's $143M spacecraft bus contract with MDA/Globalstar in February 2022 marks the largest commercial spacecraft manufacturing order in its history, confirming a revenue-generating buildup phase and providing the watchlist's first rockets-spacecraft exposure.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab selected by MDA to design and build 17 spacecraft buses for Globalstar's new LEO satellite constellation; $143M initial contract value is the largest spacecraft bus order in Rocket Lab's history, with options for up to nine additional spacecraft.
Rocket Lab Signs $143M Satellite Bus Supply Contract with MDA for Globalstar LEO constellation, marking Rocket Lab's expansion from launch provider into large-scale spacecraft bus manufacturing.

adds.rationale: Structural labor shortages from COVID-era workforce disruption are accelerating corporate automation investment across manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare, placing robotics in an early-buildup entry phase; BOTZ is a US-listed ETF launched in 2016 offering diversified exposure to this wave.

news_evidence.summary: 10 Robotics Stocks to Buy for 2022 and Beyond: labor constraints and supply-chain disruptions are pushing companies of all sizes toward industrial robots and automation systems; analysts cite 2022 as an early-buildup entry point for the robotics wave.

15 Robotics Stocks and ETFs to Watch in 2022: CMC Markets highlights BOTZ as the leading thematic ETF for industrial robotics and AI hardware, noting the global robotics market exceeded $80 billion and labor shortages are a structural driver of adoption.

adds.rationale: Germany's Zeitenwende €100B special defense fund announced February 27, 2022, combined with NATO-wide pledges to exceed the 2% GDP spending threshold, represents a durable structural inflection in global military budgets; ITA (iShares US Aerospace and Defense ETF, launched 2006) provides diversified liquid exposure to the US prime contractors most likely to capture this demand.

news_evidence.summary: German Chancellor Scholz announces Zeitenwende: a €100B off-budget special defense fund to modernize the Bundeswehr and push Germany above NATO's 2% GDP spending target — Germany's largest defense commitment since 1991 and a signal of structural, not cyclical, rearmament across the alliance.

Germany committed to improving its defense after Zeitenwende: Atlantic Council analysis confirms the €100B pledge and Germany's intent to exceed 2% GDP on defense, reversing three decades of underspending in a single parliamentary session.

rationale_overall: The Day-0 watchlist (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, SPY) saturates the AI wave bucket (four tickers, at cap) but leaves rockets-spacecraft, robotics, and geopolitical buckets completely empty. RKLB fills the rockets-spacecraft bucket at a concrete commercial milestone. BOTZ fills the robotics bucket at what the profile would call early buildup, with labor shortages providing structural fuel. ITA fills the geopolitical bucket as Germany's Zeitenwende and the broader NATO rearmament pledge — the dominant macro event of Q1 2022 — create a durable multi-year tailwind for US defense primes. Nuclear, quantum, and demographics waves have no sufficiently liquid, already-listed US ticker that meets the listing-date guardrail as of March 2022, so those remain open for future rebalances. The post-change watchlist is exactly at the max-watchlist-size cap of 8.
